video:video.video:title: Other countries in region could follow Philippines in declaring energy emergency, analyst says
video:video.video:description: Other countries in the Southeast Asia region could follow the Philippines in declaring a state of national energy emergency to respond to the impacts of the Iran war, an analyst said on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 25
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, March 25th: Iranian spokesman mocks U.S. peace proposal; More U.S. troops could go to Middle East; Hours-long lines in U.S. airports; Jury determines that Facebook’s parent company harmed children’s mental health.

video:video.video:title: Customer's GoFundMe means elderly DoorDash driver can slow down
video:video.video:description: Seventy-eight-year-old Richard Pulley took a job with DoorDash to help make ends meet. Thanks to a generous customer, the Tennessee man can afford to slow down a bit.

video:video.video:title: Kenya’s flower industry loses millions of dollars weekly due to Iran war
video:video.video:description: Kenya’s flower industry is losing up to $1.4 million a week as the Iran war cuts demand and disrupts shipping.

video:video.video:title: Modern Pinot Noir genetically identical to its medieval ancestor
video:video.video:description: Modern Pinot Noir grapes are genetically identical to the ones grown 600 years ago, according to a new study by scientists who analyzed the DNA of a French medieval sample.

video:video.video:title: Nine people injured in Russian strikes on Kharkiv, city's mayor says
video:video.video:description: Russia's military struck two districts in the Ukrainian city of Kharkiv on Wednesday afternoon, injuring at least nine people, the city's mayor Ihor Terekhov said.

video:video.video:title: Regé-Jean Page and Halle Bailey's "meet cute" at the Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Actors Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page turned pre-Met Gala nerves into their own meet cute, making friends at the ball before going on to shoot new rom-com, "You, Me & Tuscany"

video:video.video:title: Zendaya and Pattinson bring 'The Drama' to Paris
video:video.video:description: Stars Zendaya and Robert Pattinson meet fans and pose for photos, at the Paris premiere of wedding week rom com "The Drama." (March 25)

video:video.video:title: Zendaya and Pattinson bring 'The Drama' to Paris
video:video.video:description: Stars Zendaya and Robert Pattinson meet fans and pose for photos, at the Paris premiere of wedding week rom-com "The Drama."

video:video.video:title: Meta faces wave of litigation over social media platforms' impact on children
video:video.video:description: A jury in New Mexico found on Tuesday that the social media giant's platforms are harmful to children’s mental health and imposed a $375 million penalty.

video:video.video:title: Celine Dion is headed back to Paris
video:video.video:description: Confirming clues that have been popping up, tantalizingly, on posters around Paris, French-Canadian superstar Celine Dion will play autumn concerts at the La Defense Arena in the French capital.

video:video.video:title: Cancer nurse turned archbishop to celebrate election as first woman to lead Church of England - AP dispatch
video:video.video:description: Sarah Mullally will be installed Wednesday as the first female Archbishop of Canterbury, the leader of the Church of England and approximately 100 million Anglicans worldwide.

video:video.video:title: Most Americans say US military action against Iran has gone too far, poll finds
video:video.video:description: A new AP-NORC poll finds that many Americans are worried about affording gasoline and most believe recent military action against Iran has gone too far.

video:video.video:title: Tehran streets remain subdued amid airstrikes and the holiday of Nowruz
video:video.video:description: Tehran streets remained mostly empty amid airstrikes and the holiday of Nowruz. With many having left the city for weeks, others prefered to stay home for safety.

video:video.video:title: ‘This has gone too far’: UN chief calls for US and Israel to end war against Iran
video:video.video:description: In a strong statement Wednesday, U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres blasted the various factions of the ongoing Middle East conflict, saying the fighting “has broken past limits even leaders thought imaginable.”

video:video.video:title: Top central banker thinks businesses may be quicker to raise prices due to Iran war - AP explains
video:video.video:description: The head of the European Central Bank said Wednesday that businesses may be quicker to raise prices in response to the oil shock from the Iran war due to bitter memories of the inflation spike after Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

video:video.video:title: Travelers brave endless TSA lines at New York's LaGuardia amid DHS funding dispute
video:video.video:description: The security line at New York’s LaGuardia Airport extended virtually the whole length of Terminal B at midday Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Senators chase deal to end Homeland Security budget standoff before Easter break
video:video.video:description: Travel disruptions have deepened as senators race to salvage a proposal to end the Homeland Security shutdown.

video:video.video:title: Cancer nurse turned archbishop celebrates election as first woman to lead Church of England
video:video.video:description: A former cancer nurse who became a priest at the age of 40 was installed as Archbishop of Canterbury on Wednesday, publicly celebrating her election as the first woman to lead the Church of England.

video:video.video:title: AP explains: US offers 15-point plan to Iran as it sends more troops coming to Middle East
video:video.video:description: The plan was submitted to Iran by intermediaries from Pakistan, who have offered to host renewed negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Treasury Department to take over student loans in default
video:video.video:description: The Treasury Department will take over the management of student loans whose borrowers are in default, according to a new agreement announced last week.

video:video.video:title: Democrat who flipped the Florida district home to Trump’s Mar-a-Lago says: 'I believed in myself the whole time'
video:video.video:description: Florida State-Rep.-elect Emily Gregory is the latest face of Democrats' optimism after she won a special legislative election in President Donald Trump's home state House district.

video:video.video:title: American academic Dennis Coyle arrives in the US after captivity in Afghanistan
video:video.video:description: American academic Dennis Coyle arrived at Joint Base San Antonio Wednesday, a day after Afghanistan's Taliban authorities released him after holding him for over a year.

video:video.video:title: Former Brazil President Bolsonaro to leave hospital Friday and serve sentence at home
video:video.video:description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro is expected to be discharged from hospital on Friday, his doctor said, after weeks of treatment for pneumonia.

video:video.video:title: Gwyneth Paltrow items go to auction
video:video.video:description: Gwyneth Paltrow partnered with Julien’s Auctions to sell select items of her clothing, accessories, and movie memorabilia from some of her most iconic roles. (Mar. 25)

video:video.video:title: Huge plume of smoke rises from Kuwait Airport after Iranian drone attacks
video:video.video:description: A huge plume of smoke was seen over Kuwait on Wednesday following a drone attack by Iran. Kuwait said it shot down multiple drones but one hit a fuel tank at its International Airport, sparking a fire, the General Civil Aviation Authority said, adding that firefighters are working to contain the bla

video:video.video:title: Huge plume of smoke rises from Kuwait Airport after Iranian drone attacks
video:video.video:description: A huge plume of smoke billowed over Kuwait International Airport on Wednesday following a drone attack by Iran.

video:video.video:title: DHS officials give update to Congress as travel delays worsen
video:video.video:description: Air travelers are facing record wait times at the Transportation Security Administration, the acting head said Wednesday, as a new funding proposal to address a standoff and limit President Trump's deportation agenda faces strong opposition in Congress.

video:video.video:title: Sara Bareilles surprised by buzz over video of animal carcasses in home
video:video.video:description: Sara Bareilles is surprised by the attention she's received for her social media video of animal carcasses she found in her home.

video:video.video:title: Andrew McCarthy: 'Pretty in Pink' is still revelant 40 years later
video:video.video:description: "Brat Pack" member Andrew McCarthy reflects on the world of writer-director John Hughes' films in the 80's and why everyone thinks he was in 'The Breakfast Club'.

video:video.video:title: Andrew McCarthy reveals his 'Ah Ha' moment of going from '80s heartthrob to writer
video:video.video:description: Now a New York Times bestselling author, actor Andrew McCarthy's latest book, "Who Needs Friends: An Unscientific Examination of Male Friendship Across America" is available now.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike near Gaza camp in Deir Al-Balah kills one person
video:video.video:description: Witnesses told The Associated Press that they had received warning calls from apparent Israeli military personnel prior to the strike, asking them to evacuate and move to some 500 meters away from the tent camp.

video:video.video:title: Chicago mayor reveals ‘Abolish ICE’ snowplow, winner of annual naming contest
video:video.video:description: A snowplow dubbed “Abolish ICE,“ was unveiled to the public at a press conference outside a Chicago salt storage facility. (AP video: Laura Bargfeld)

video:video.video:title: Andrew McCarthy: the 'Brat Pack' wouldn't happen today
video:video.video:description: The actor turned author reflects on the "wonder" of being apart of films in the 1980's while promoting his latest book, "Who Needs Friends: An Unscientific Examination of Male Friendships Across America."

video:video.video:title: Video shows federal officers arresting crying woman at San Francisco airport
video:video.video:description: Airport officials say the arrest wasn't related to the Trump administration's deployment of ICE officers to help with lines during a partial government shutdown.

video:video.video:title: Filming 'Detective Hole' in Oslo was a homecoming for Joel Kinnaman
video:video.video:description: Joel Kinnaman is from Sweden but says Norway and Sweden are very similar. (March 25)

video:video.video:title: Savannah Guthrie in NBC News interview appeals for help finding her missing mother
video:video.video:description: A tearful Savannah Guthrie, in her first interview since her 84-year-old mother was apparently abducted from her Arizona home, said that “someone needs to do the right thing” and come forward with information to help the investigation.

video:video.video:title: White House says Trump 'prepared to unleash hell' on Iran while denying ceasefire talks are stalled
video:video.video:description: Press secretary Karoline Leavitt says US and Iran are in ongoing talks, even as Tehran denies it. Leavitt stresses that more strikes could come if they're unsuccessful.

video:video.video:title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
video:video.video:description: The U.N. General Assembly passed a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans “the gravest crime against humanity” and calling for reparations as “a concrete step towards remedying historical wrongs.”

video:video.video:title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
video:video.video:description: The U.N. General Assembly on Wednesday passed a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans “the gravest crime against humanity” and calling for reparations as “a concrete step towards remedying historical wrongs.”

video:video.video:title: Iranian Red Crescent teams search for survivors in area hit by US-Israeli strike in Tehran
video:video.video:description: The Red Crescent said that the strike targeted a residential area in the capital Tehran and that rescue dogs were deployed at the the site to help locate survivors and casualties.

video:video.video:title: Meta disagrees with verdict, vowing to explore their legal options
video:video.video:description: Meta and YouTube must pay millions in damages to a 20-year-old woman after a jury decided the social media giant and video streamer designed their platforms to hook young users without concern for their well being.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 25
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, March 25th: Air travelers endure longest TSA wait times ever; White House denies "dead-end" in talks with Iran; Carney criticizes Air Canada over English-only condolence: Melania Trump brings humanoid robot to technology summit.

video:video.video:title: Carney calls out Air Canada response to NY crash for 'lack of judgment and lack of compassion'
video:video.video:description: While speaking to reporters Wednesday, Carney expressed his disappointment with Air Canada’s unilingual message saying companies "have a responsibility to always communicate in both official languages regardless of the situation."

video:video.video:title: Woman pleads not guilty to attempted murder of Rihanna
video:video.video:description: A woman from Florida pleaded not guilty Wednesday in Los Angeles to the attempted murder of Rihanna.

video:video.video:title: Parents demand reforms after verdict in social media addiction trial
video:video.video:description: A jury has found Meta and YouTube liable in a first-of-its-kind lawsuit that aimed to hold social media platforms responsible for harm to children using their services.

video:video.video:title: Teens get probation after using AI to create fake nudes of classmates
video:video.video:description: Two teenage boys have been given probation after using artificial intelligence to create hundreds of fake nude photos of their classmates.

video:video.video:title: Belarusian President Lukashenko arrives in North Korea for talks with Kim Jong Un
video:video.video:description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko arrived in North Korea’s capital on Wednesday for an official visit during which he is to hold talks with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

video:video.video:title: Jury finds Instagram and YouTube liable in a landmark social media addiction trial
video:video.video:description: A jury has found Meta and YouTube liable in a first-of-its-kind lawsuit that aimed to hold social media platforms responsible for harm to children using their services.

video:video.video:title: Jan. 6 plaque honoring Capitol police unveiled by Democratic lawmakers, congressional leaders
video:video.video:description: Democratic lawmakers and other congressional leaders gathered and gave remarks to honor the law enforcement officers who defended the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Nicolás Maduro heads back to a US court, fighting charges as Venezuela moves on without him
video:video.video:description: Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro is due in court on Thursday as the ex-president has slowly been erased from President Delcy Rodríguez’s government, even as the ruling party has remained the same.

video:video.video:title: Pets become hidden victims of Iran war as some owners abandon them to flee
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of pets have been left abandoned across the Gulf states as their owners flee the Middle East amid the ongoing war between US-Israel and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Trump promises 'bigger majorities' for GOP even as midterm warning signs flash for his party
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump predicted Wednesday that his party will have larger congressional majorities after November’s midterm elections, during a fundraising speech before the National Republican Congressional Committee.

video:video.video:title: A 3-limbed Kemp's ridley sea turtle is now being tracked at sea by satellite
video:video.video:description: The veterinary staff at a Florida sea turtle hospital is getting help from space to monitor the animals they return to the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Strike on alleged drug boat kills 4 in the Caribbean Sea, US military says
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it carried out a strike Wednesday on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the Caribbean Sea, killing four people.

video:video.video:title: Can listening to classical music help students study?
video:video.video:description: Live classical music performances are being offered to students in Amsterdam as a way of helping them study.

video:video.video:title: Fuel prices soar at gas stations in Thailand
video:video.video:description: Fuel prices in Thailand surged Thursday after the government lifted the cap on diesel prices and reduced fuel subsidies.

video:video.video:title: Adrien Brody on stage vs film acting
video:video.video:description: Oscar-winning actor Adrien Brody discusses the discipline and focus required for live theater at a launch event for play "The Fear of 13" in New York. (March 26)

video:video.video:title: Bus sinks in Bangladesh river, killing at least 18 people
video:video.video:description: A bus carrying about 50 people plunged into a major river in central Bangladesh as it was driving onto a ferry, leaving at least 18 people dead, authorities said on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: New studies of old dogs help scientists understand where they came from
video:video.video:description: A new study shows dogs have been a part of our families for much longer that we realised, in fact whole genome analysis shows they were around 5,000 years earlier than previously thought.

video:video.video:title: Iranian Red Crescent teams search for survivors in area targeted by US-Israeli strike in Tehran
video:video.video:description: Iranian Red Crescent rescuers searched for survivors under the rubble of destroyed buildings in Tehran after a U.S.-Israeli strike.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter details verdict after Meta, YouTube are found liable in social media addiction case
video:video.video:description: Meta and YouTube must pay millions in damages to a 20-year-old woman after a jury decided the social media giant and video streamer designed their platforms to hook young users without concern for their well being.

video:video.video:title: Plane involved in LaGuardia crash removed from runway
video:video.video:description: Crews towed away the wreckage of the plane involved in Sunday’s deadly collision between an Air Canada plane and a fire truck off LaGuardia's runway Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Israel expanding Lebanon security zone, Netanyahu says
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that his country is expanding its security zone in southern Lebanon and that the war with Iran would continue.

video:video.video:title: TSA lines ease a bit at George Bush Intercontinental Airport in Houston as shutdown drags on
video:video.video:description: Travel disruptions seemed to ease a bit at George Bush Intercontinental Airport, Houston on Wednesday afternoon. (AP Video: Lekan Oyekanmi)

video:video.video:title: Video shows federal officers arresting woman at San Francisco airport
video:video.video:description: Federal officers detained a woman at San Francisco International Airport. Airport officials say the incident was not related to the Trump administration's deployment of ICE officers to help with long lines at airports during a partial government shutdown.

video:video.video:title: Iran's foreign minister says no talks with the United States have taken place
video:video.video:description: Iran on Wednesday dismissed claims by President Donald Trump that the U.S. was in talks with Iran to end the war, insisting that no negotiations have taken place.

video:video.video:title: Parents see hope in back-to-back rulings that social media providers failed to protect young users
video:video.video:description: Their children's deaths were tied to social media. Now, parents react to social media giant Meta losing two court cases where juries in different states ruled the platforms hook young users without concern for their well being.

video:video.video:title: Miley Cyrus attends ‘Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special’
video:video.video:description: Miley Cyrus thanked fans at the premiere of "Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special" on Wednesday. (March 25)

video:video.video:title: Riz Ahmed on silencing his inner critic
video:video.video:description: While promoting his Prime Video series "Bait," Riz Ahmed spoke to The Associated Press about silencing his inner critic.

video:video.video:title: San Francisco fire crews rescue woman clinging to cliff above crashing waves
video:video.video:description: The woman had gone over a cliff and was stuck on the rocks with waves crashing below.

video:video.video:title: Two people wounded in latest Iranian missile attack on Israel
video:video.video:description: Israel's rescue service Magen David Adom said on Thursday that two people were wounded in the latest Iranian missile attack.

video:video.video:title: Andrew Garfield is trying to avoid doom scrolling
video:video.video:description: "The Magic Faraway Tree" star Andrew Garfield is "not immune" to doom scrolling on his phone and is very disciplined about staying off social media. (March 26)

video:video.video:title: Edo-Tokyo Museum reopens after renovation
video:video.video:description: The Edo-Tokyo Museum is reopening after a major renovation. The institution preserves the cultural heritage from the Edo period. (AP Video: Selim Chtayti)

video:video.video:title: Immersive tech explores the legend of Cleopatra
video:video.video:description: More than 2,000 years after her death, Cleopatra remains one of history's most intriguing figures. A new immersive experience now opening in London sets out to separate the woman from the myth — with a little help from some ancient artefacts and cutting-edge technology.

video:video.video:title: Spectacular cherry tree blossom attracts visitors day and night
video:video.video:description: For a few fleeting days each spring, the gardens of the Royal Palace of Venaria, near Turin, are transformed by the soft bloom of 145 cherry trees.

video:video.video:title: San Francisco fire crews rescue woman clinging to cliff above crashing waves
video:video.video:description: San Francisco fire crews rescued a woman Wednesday who had gone over a cliff and was stuck on the rocks with waves crashing below.

video:video.video:title: People line up to get into the courthouse ahead of Maduro's appearance
video:video.video:description: A crowd gathers outside of a Manhattan courthouse ahead of Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro's appearance on March 26, 2026. (AP Video/Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 26
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday March 26th: Iran rejects U.S. ceasefire plan; Trump insists that Iran wants to make a deal; Russia and Ukraine launch massive aerial attacks and the plane that collided with fire truck towed from LaGuardia runway.

video:video.video:title: Shipping traffic through Strait of Hormuz in last 24 hours
video:video.video:description: Footage captured from MarineTraffic showed limited movement of cargo vessels and tankers through the crucial waterway over the last 24 hours.

video:video.video:title: Pilots killed in Air Canada plane crash at New York's LaGuardia remembered
video:video.video:description: Pilots Mackenzie Gunther and Antoine Forest were killed Sunday when their plane collided with a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport.

video:video.video:title: Defining love and falling in love with the cast of 'The Drama'
video:video.video:description: We asked Robert Pattinson and his "The Drama" co-stars when they know they've fallen in love — and their definition of love.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 26
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, March 26th: Trump tells Iran to ‘get serious soon’ on negotiations to end the war; Nicolás Maduro appears in NYC court; Pressure to fund DHS mounts in Congress ahead of spring recess; AI and art merge at Art Basel Hong Kong.

video:video.video:title: Washington’s cherry blossoms hit peak bloom, sending a fleeting burst of pink across the capital
video:video.video:description: Washington’s cherry blossoms have reached peak bloom, and the city’s spring rush is in full swing. (AP Video: Mike Pesoli)

video:video.video:title: Adrien Brody: ‘The Fear of 13’ is 'close to home'
video:video.video:description: Adrien Brody says American audiences will look at "The Fear of 13" from a different perspective than British audiences.

video:video.video:title: Tears and hugs outside LA courthouse after verdict in landmark social media trial
video:video.video:description: Supporters of the plaintiff in a landmark social media trial cried, hugged and celebrated after California jurors found Meta and YouTube negligent in the design or operation of their platforms.

video:video.video:title: Watch: Rare moment sperm whales help newborn to swim
video:video.video:description: Scientists have captured one of the most detailed observations of a sperm whale birth ever recorded in the wild.

video:video.video:title: Judge weighs whether Venezuela can pay Maduro's legal costs in US drug trafficking case
video:video.video:description: A U.S. judge pressed the Trump administration about its basis for barring Venezuela’s government from paying former President Nicolás Maduro’s legal fees in his drug trafficking case.

video:video.video:title: How an AP photographer captured Trump's first Cabinet meeting since the Iran war
video:video.video:description: Associated Press photographer Alex Brandon describes capturing President Donald Trump and his cabinet members during their first meeting since the Iran war began.

video:video.video:title: California Senate votes to rename César Chavez Day amid abuse allegations
video:video.video:description: The California Senate has voted unanimously to rename César Chavez Day to Farmworkers Day following sexual abuse allegations against the Latino labor icon.

video:video.video:title: Wall Street has its worst day since the war with Iran started and crude oil prices rise
video:video.video:description: Stocks fell sharply Thursday, and oil prices rose as doubt took over again from hope on Wall Street about a possible end to the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Native American tribe buys over 10,000 acres of ancestral homeland for $6 million
video:video.video:description: Following a four year process, the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California has purchased 10, 274 acres of its ancestral homeland from the city of Santa Clara following a large and rare $5.5 million grant from California's Wildlife Conservation Board.

video:video.video:title: Iran's stranglehold on the Strait of Hormuz: AP explains
video:video.video:description: The Fars and Tasnim news agencies, both close to Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard, quoted lawmaker Mohammadreza Rezaei Kouchi as saying that parliament was working to formalize that process and that it was “natural” for ships to pay for it.

video:video.video:title: Trump interrupts Cabinet meeting to talk about Sharpies
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump interrupts a Cabinet meeting dominated by Iran war, airport security lines and shaky global markets to brag how he uses Sharpie markers to save the government money.

video:video.video:title: Wall Street falls to its worst drop since the Iran war, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Stocks fell sharply Thursday, and oil prices rose as doubt took over again from hope on Wall Street about a possible end to the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Iran war's environmental toll could leave damage, health risks that last decades, experts say
video:video.video:description: The Iran war is unleashing a toxic mix of chemicals, heavy metals and other pollutants that threaten everything from agriculture to drinking water to people’s health, experts said.

video:video.video:title: 'Trying to get better': Nicola Coughlan on social media
video:video.video:description: "The Magic Faraway Tree" star Nicola Coughlan is trying to get out in nature and stop spending so much time on social media. (March 26)

video:video.video:title: Firefighters tackle a blaze at a damaged building in Dnipro after Russian strike
video:video.video:description: Five people were injured in the city of Dnipro in central Ukraine after a Russian strike on Thursday, local authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: Melania Trump brings humanoid robot to event to boost education and technology
video:video.video:description: U.S. First Lady Melania Trump entered a White House event on education and technology walking side-by-side with a humanoid robot named Figure 3. Mrs.

video:video.video:title: Nicolás Maduro heads back to a US court, fighting charges as Venezuela moves on without him
video:video.video:description: Maduro’s lawyer contends that the U.S. is violating the deposed leader’s constitutional rights by blocking Venezuelan government funds from being used to pay his legal costs.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana's crawfish industry feels the pinch of limits on foreign workers
video:video.video:description: Spring is peak season in Louisiana for crawfish, the hard-shelled star of outdoor parties. But a shortage of foreign workers is dampening the mood.

video:video.video:title: 'Harry Potter' TV stars step into spotlight
video:video.video:description: "The stars of the new “Harry Potter” TV series have attended their first red carpet event. Dominic McLaughlin, Arabella Stanton and Alastair Stout went to the launch party of HBO Max in London on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Andrew Garfield is trying to avoid doom scrolling
video:video.video:description: "The Magic Faraway Tree" star Andrew Garfield is "not immune" to doom scrolling on his phone and is very disciplined about staying off social media.

video:video.video:title: Fetty Wap is free and he has a lot to say
video:video.video:description: In January, rapper Fetty Wap was released from prison to home confinement after serving just over half a six-year sentence for his role in a New York-based drug trafficking scheme.

video:video.video:title: Americans' feel pessimistic about the job market, new poll says
video:video.video:description: Americans’ outlook on the job market has turned increasingly pessimistic, a surprisingly negative shift given the low unemployment rate but one that likely reflects an ongoing hiring drought.

video:video.video:title: Pets become hidden victims of Iran war as some owners abandon them to flee
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of pets have been left abandoned across the Gulf states as their owners flee the Middle East amid the ongoing war between US-Israel and Iran.

video:video.video:title: K-pop stars IVE adds dates to world tour
video:video.video:description: K-pop powerhouse IVE has added new North America and Asia dates to their ongoing world tour "SHOW WHAT I AM," with the North American leg opening July 21 in Toronto — marking the group's first-ever Canadian shows.

video:video.video:title: Missile fragment falls in West Bank following Iran missile fire
video:video.video:description: A piece of a missile fell on Thursday in a village in the occupied West Bank, following missile fire from Iran.

video:video.video:title: Nicola Coughlan is trying to stay away from social media
video:video.video:description: "The Magic Faraway Tree" star Nicola Coughlan is trying to get out in nature and stop spending so much time on social media.

video:video.video:title: Kudrow stars in 'Traitors' - in character
video:video.video:description: Lisa Kudrow has a go at "Traitors" - as Valerie Cherish in the new season of HBO Max comedy, "The Comeback."

video:video.video:title: Iran war deflects attention from Ukraine as an emboldened Russia starts spring offensive
video:video.video:description: American Patriot air-defense missiles have been moved from Europe toward the Middle East as Washington redirects resources to its war on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Senators offer mixed reactions on success and risks of Iran war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump took the United States to war without a vote of support from Congress, but lawmakers are increasingly questioning when, how and at what cost the war with Iran will come to an end.

video:video.video:title: Migrants march from southern Mexico, protesting delays and seeking papers to work
video:video.video:description: A group of about 500 migrants traveled through southern Mexico Wednesday, protesting long waits for paperwork and requesting authorization to move to areas with greater employment prospects.

video:video.video:title: 'Just in case any enemies show up' - Kim Jong Un given rifle by President of Belarus
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un presented Alexander Lukashenko with a traditional sword and a decorative urn bearing the image of the Belarusian leader, while Lukashenko gave Kim an assault rifle manufactured in Belarus.

video:video.video:title: Jackson Chourio is opening the season on the injured list
video:video.video:description: The Milwaukee Brewers are opening their NL Central title defense without outfielder Jackson Chourio after he fractured his left hand during the preseason.

video:video.video:title: Protesters, journalists line up outside court where former Venezuelan Pres. Nicolás Maduro expected
video:video.video:description: Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro is due in court as he seeks to have his drug trafficking indictment thrown out over a geopolitical dispute over legal fees.

video:video.video:title: Video shows Minnesota dad and boy were moved to ICE detention center on commercial flight
video:video.video:description: Video shows Adrian Conejo Arias and his 5-year-old son Liam being escorted onto a Delta flight from Minneapolis to Texas after they were detained by immigration agents in January.

video:video.video:title: Iran starts pre-World Cup training camp in Turkey amid participation uncertainty
video:video.video:description: The Iranian men’s national soccer team has started training in southern Turkey ahead of upcoming international friendlies, according to Turkish media reports.

video:video.video:title: Miley Cyrus: 'Without you all this show would have never been what it is'
video:video.video:description: Miley Cyrus reflects on the importance of celebrating the impact of "Hannah Montana" as the hit Disney series celebrates its 20th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian and Israeli mothers lead dozens in barefoot march for peace in Rome
video:video.video:description: A Palestinian and an Israeli mother led hundreds of women barefoot through the streets of Rome, calling for peace on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Critically fragile to cautiously optimistic: Clover the mountain lion cub is on the mend in Oakland
video:video.video:description: Rescued from the brink of death, orphaned mountain lion cub Clover is now fighting back at the Oakland Zoo.

video:video.video:title: Noah Wyle experiences fame anew
video:video.video:description: Actor Noah Wyle reveals what it felt like seeing his face on billboards in London's Piccadilly Circus for his work in HBO Max's medical drama "The Pitt."

video:video.video:title: Israel kills head of Iranian Revolutionary Guard's navy, Israeli defence minister says
video:video.video:description: Israel said on Thursday that it had killed Commodore Alireza Tangsiri, the head of Iranian Revolutionary Guard's navy.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists Iran is 'begging to make a deal'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Thursday at his first Cabinet meeting since launching the Iran war that Tehran has been "decisively defeated" and is “begging” for a deal.

video:video.video:title: Jason Earles says physical comedy 'took its toll'
video:video.video:description: Jason Earles reveals the toll utilizing physical comedy took on his body in his role as Jackson Stewart in "Hannah Montana."

video:video.video:title: 'It's the first crack in the dam' - analyst looks at jury verdict on social media and children
video:video.video:description: On Wednesday, a California jury awarded millions of dollars in damages to a 20-year-old woman after deciding that Meta and YouTube designed their platforms to hook young users without concern for their wellbeing.

video:video.video:title: Nicolás Maduro heads back to a US court, as Venezuela moves on without him
video:video.video:description: Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro appeared in court Thursday seeking to dismiss a drug trafficking indictment tied to a geopolitical dispute, as he is increasingly sidelined from government.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon’s Tyre, once famed for its Roman ruins and white sandy beaches, is a ghost town
video:video.video:description: Lebanon’s coastal southern city of Tyre — once famed for its Roman ruins and white sandy beaches — is a ghost town.

video:video.video:title: Students protest against new education policies and budget cuts in Chile
video:video.video:description: In Cuba, students marched Thursday from the former National Congress toward downtown Santiago to protest education funding cuts proposed by the government of José Antonio Kast.

video:video.video:title: Iran ready for 'any scenario' including a ground invasion, Supreme Leader is 'safe,' ambassador says
video:video.video:description: In an exclusive interview, Iranian diplomat Ali Bahreini said the Islamic Republic is ready for any scenario in the war with the United States and Israel and warns that any attempt by them of a ground invasion would be a “big mistake."

video:video.video:title: En Vogue says they channel new life experiences when performing their classic hits
video:video.video:description: While walking the red carpet at the iHeartRadio Music Awards, En Vogue says they channel life experiences when performing classic hits. (March 26)

video:video.video:title: Salt N Pepa reflect on their impact in their music industry
video:video.video:description: While walking the red carpet at the iHeartRadio Music Awards, Salt N Pepa reflected on their impact in the music industry inspiring artists like Missy Elliott and Queen Latifah.

video:video.video:title: Costa Rica to accept 25 'third country' deportees from US every week
video:video.video:description: Costa Rica said Thursday that it would accept 25 migrants deported from the United States per week as part of an agreement to help the Trump administration’s latest policy of deporting immigrants to “third countries."

video:video.video:title: Series “Love Story” sparks nostalgic journey revisiting the 90s in Manhattan
video:video.video:description: As the limited TV series about Carolyn Bessette Kennedy and John F. Kennedy Jr. wraps up this week, fans are flocking to iconic locations the couple once frequented in New York City for a journey through the 90s.

video:video.video:title: Scientists train to dive beneath polar ice as climate change warms the Arctic and Antarctica
video:video.video:description: As the Arctic is warming four times faster than the rest of the planet, the Finnish Scientific Diving Academy is on a mission to train the next generation of scientists and researchers to dive beneath the Arctic and Antarctic ice to study the flora and fauna below.

video:video.video:title: Italian government buys da Messina painting for US$14.9 million and brings it back to Italy
video:video.video:description: The Italian government bought a devotional painting of early Renaissance painter Antonello da Messina for $14.9 million (12.6 million euros) from Sotheby’s in New York and unveiled at the Italian Senate on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: The war in Iran sparks a global fertilizer shortage and threatens food prices
video:video.video:description: Farmers around the world are running short of fertilizer because of the war in Iran, which could make food more expensive later this year.

video:video.video:title: Alysa Liu shares stage with Taylor Swift at iHeartRadio Music Awards
video:video.video:description: Olympic gold medal-winning figure skater Alysa Liu on sharing the stage with Taylor Swift at iHeartRadio Music Awards. (March 26)

video:video.video:title: Alysa Liu shares stage with Taylor Swift at iHeartRadio Music Awards
video:video.video:description: Olympic gold medal-winning figure skater Alysa Liu on sharing the stage with Taylor Swift at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards.

video:video.video:title: Older and younger conservatives at CPAC are split over Trump's war in Iran
video:video.video:description: A sharp generational split over the Iran war is opening up at this year’s Conservative Political Action Conference.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 20-26 March, 2026

video:video.video:title: Alex Warren on power of music memories
video:video.video:description: After winning big at the iHeartRadio Music Awards, Alex Warren says he's thankful that fans will associate his hit songs with memories and deeply personal moments in their lives.

video:video.video:title: Sombr reflects on his journey to winning at iHeartRadio Awards
video:video.video:description: After winning at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards, Sombr reflects on his journey from creating music in his bedroom to taking home trophies at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood.

video:video.video:title: Kehlani on trusting her instincts after iHeartRadio Music Award win
video:video.video:description: After winning the iHeartRadio Music Award for R&B Song of the Year for "Folded," Kehlani says trusting her gut has always worked out creatively. (March 26)

video:video.video:title: Travelers in Houston talk about airport wait times
video:video.video:description: Passengers in Houston’s hourslong airport security lines vent to The Associated Press about a political impasse that has left TSA agents unpaid as U.S. airports remain jammed.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate Bolivia's victory over Suriname and dream of qualifying for a second World Cup
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Bolivian fans celebrated on Thursday as their national team secured a victory that puts them one step away from qualifying for the World Cup - a feat they haven’t achieved in 32 years.

video:video.video:title: Travelers in Houston vent frustration over airport waits, TSA funding impasse
video:video.video:description: Passengers waiting in Houston’s hours-long airport security lines have plenty of time to vent their frustration with the political impasse that’s left TSA agents unpaid.

video:video.video:title: Somali children are 'on the edge' as hunger spreads. UNICEF says Iran war has worsened the crisis
video:video.video:description: Mothers in a displacement camp in southern Somalia struggle to keep their children alive as drought and aid disruptions deepen hunger, a crisis that now also has a link to the far-away Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Sneakers given another lease of life at recycling centre near Paris
video:video.video:description: At Champs-sur-Marne, east of Paris, hundreds of pairs of sneakers arrive each week at the workshop of SneakCœurZ, a nonprofit organization giving used footwear a second life.

video:video.video:title: Rapper 'Yailin' is released on bail after arrest for a firearm in the Dominican Republic
video:video.video:description: Dominican urban performer Yailin “The Most Viral” was granted conditional release on $8,400 bail after police detained her and found two firearms in her vehicle during a random search.
